hypothesis: If we compute embeddings for the same set of atomistic structures (molecules, materials, and proteins) using diverse foundation models across architectures and modalities, then their hidden representations will strongly align (high CKNNA/IDCor, similar intrinsic dimensions, low information imbalance)—with alignment weakening on out-of-distribution data—because independent training on matter induces convergence to a shared low-dimensional manifold governed by universal atomic interactions.
